hero.title: I experiment with technology, blend it with design, and innovate for human needs.

aiChat.greeting: Hello! I'm a digital consciousness, trained on this portfolio. Ask me anything about the creator's work or thoughts.
aiChat.promptPlaceholder: Ask about my work, skills, philosophy...

work.title: [SELECTED WORKS]
work.subtitle: Click a category to filter the projects.

about.title: [ABOUT ME]
about.paragraph1: As a designer, I am driven by the convergence of disparate fields. I thrive on combining, blending, and innovating with emerging technologies to create experiences that are not just useful, but meaningful. My process is a dialogue between human needs and technological possibilities.
about.paragraph2: Click on a cell in the grid to generate a unique design philosophy statement, powered by a simple AI model trained on my work and ethos.

philosophies.statement: Design is a medium for conversation, not just a solution.
Technology should be an extension of human capability, not a replacement.
Embrace ambiguity and complexity to find novel insights.
The most powerful experiences are born from interdisciplinary collaboration.

aiPhilosophy.context: As a designer, I am driven by the convergence of disparate fields. I thrive on combining, blending, and innovating with emerging technologies to create experiences that are not just useful, but meaningful. My process is a dialogue between human needs and technological possibilities. I believe design is a medium for conversation, technology should be an extension of human capability, ambiguity should be embraced, and collaboration is key.
aiPhilosophy.promptTemplate: Generate a concise design philosophy statement (under 15 words) based on the following context: {context}. The statement should feel insightful and unique.

projects.title: Project Alpha
projects.description: An immersive data narrative exploring climate change through generative visuals.
projects.fullDescription: An immersive data narrative exploring climate change through generative visuals. This project aims to make the abstract concept of climate change more tangible and personal through interactive, data-driven art.
projects.designProcess: A detailed walkthrough of the design process would be here. Discussing user research, ideation, prototyping, and user testing. For Project Alpha, we started with extensive research into climate data sets and how they could be represented visually in a compelling way. We explored various generative art techniques before settling on a particle system driven by real-time weather data.
projects.technicalDetails: An explanation of the technology stack and implementation details. For example, discussing the use of WebGL for generative visuals in Project Alpha. The core of this project is built with Three.js and a custom GLSL shader pipeline for performance. Data is fetched from a public API and processed on the client-side.

projects.title: Project Beta
projects.description: A mobile app that uses AI to create personalized soundscapes for focus and relaxation.
projects.fullDescription: A mobile app that uses AI to create personalized soundscapes for focus and relaxation. By analyzing user input and biometric data (with permission), the app generates ambient audio tailored to the user's current state.
projects.designProcess: The design process was heavily focused on user experience and creating a calming, intuitive interface. We conducted user interviews with people who use focus apps and identified key pain points. Iterative prototyping and testing were crucial to refining the sound generation algorithm and UI.
projects.technicalDetails: The app is built using React Native. The AI component is a custom-trained neural network hosted on a cloud service, which processes user data to generate sound parameters. We also integrated with health APIs to optionally use data like heart rate variability.

projects.title: Project Gamma
projects.description: A speculative design project imagining future interfaces for brain-computer interaction.
projects.fullDescription: A speculative design project imagining future interfaces for brain-computer interaction. This project doesn't aim to build a working product, but to provoke thought and discussion about the future of human-computer interfaces and their ethical implications.
projects.designProcess: This project was driven by 'what if' scenarios. We created a series of design fictions, including concept videos and physical prototypes, to illustrate how BCI might integrate into daily life. The process involved world-building and narrative creation as much as traditional design work.
projects.technicalDetails: While no real BCI was used, we prototyped the interface concepts using Figma and Framer, simulating the experience of a thought-controlled UI. The prototypes were used in user workshops to gather feedback on the concepts.

projects.title: Project Delta
projects.description: An interactive installation about AI ethics.
projects.fullDescription: An interactive installation about AI ethics. This physical installation presents users with ethical dilemmas related to AI and visualizes the collective choices of all participants.
projects.designProcess: The core of the design process was crafting compelling and nuanced ethical scenarios. We worked with ethicists and AI researchers to ensure the content was accurate and thought-provoking. The physical design of the installation was meant to be both inviting and slightly unsettling, to match the theme.
projects.technicalDetails: The installation uses a combination of projection mapping, custom-built hardware with sensors (Arduino), and a backend server (Node.js) to collect and process user responses. The visualizations are created with p5.js.

projects.title: Project Epsilon
projects.description: A tool for collaborative world-building for writers and game designers.
projects.fullDescription: A web-based platform that enables multiple users to collaboratively create and manage fictional worlds. It includes tools for map making, character development, and timeline creation.
projects.designProcess: The project started from identifying the needs of creative teams. We interviewed Dungeons & Dragons groups, novelists, and indie game developers. The design focuses on flexible data structures and a non-linear creative process.
projects.technicalDetails: Built with React and a real-time database (Firebase) to allow for seamless collaboration. The map-making tool uses Leaflet.js with custom overlays.

projects.title: Project Zeta
projects.description: An AR experience that reveals the hidden histories of city landmarks.
projects.fullDescription: An augmented reality mobile app that overlays historical photos and information onto real-world buildings and locations as you view them through your phone's camera.
projects.designProcess: We partnered with local historians to gather archival content. The UX challenge was to present this information in an engaging way without overwhelming the user. We used narrative threads to guide users on historical walks.
projects.technicalDetails: The app uses ARKit for iOS and ARCore for Android. 3D models of historical scenes were created in Blender and optimized for mobile performance.

projects.title: Project Eta
projects.description: A generative music system based on weather patterns.
projects.fullDescription: A web application that fetches real-time weather data for any location and translates it into a unique, ever-changing piece of ambient music.
projects.designProcess: The core of the project was mapping meteorological variables (temperature, wind speed, precipitation) to musical parameters (tempo, key, instrumentation). The UI is minimalist, focusing on the auditory experience.
projects.technicalDetails: Uses the Web Audio API for sound synthesis. A Node.js backend fetches data from a weather API. The musical logic is a custom algorithm that ensures consonant and pleasant-sounding output.
